name: Acarbose extended HET3 mouse lifespan with stronger male effects
summary: A repeated ITP acarbose study provides robust animal lifespan support for glucose-handling and insulin-axis modulation, with an important sex-dependent boundary.
statement: In genetically heterogeneous mice tested across three sites, dietary acarbose significantly changed lifespan at 400, 1000, and 2500 ppm, with 16-17% median-longevity increases in males and 4-5% increases in females at the two higher doses.
quantitative_note: Age at the 90th percentile increased 8-11% in males at each dose and 3% in females only at 1000 ppm.
caveats: This is animal lifespan evidence, not human aging evidence.
The effect was materially larger in male mice than female mice.
Acarbose is approved for diabetes, but this study does not establish preventive geroscience value in humans.
